title: castilleja rupicola
intro: Castilleja rupicola, also known as mountain Indian paintbrush, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of surrounding plants. The roots of Castilleja rupicola contain various alkaloids, including emetine and cephaline, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to offer health benefits including immune system support and relief from respiratory issues.
